【説明会】ハードウェアスタートアップ向けアクセラレーションプログラム 「Forge」

Aug 28, 2025

Event

Who we are

Program Overview

Program Overview

What is Forge ?

Forge is a global hardware accelerator backed by the Tokyo Metropolitan Government’s TIB CATAPULT program. We pair Japan’s manufacturing strength with committed corporate partners to move hardware startups from prototype to proof and into production—combining co‑funded prototyping, a partner‑validated PoC pathway (subject to partner approval and team/product readiness), and investor access, supported by veteran mentors, fab access, and rigorous DFM (Design for Manufacturability). Designed for seed stage and beyond, Forge concentrates support into two phases—prep and build.

Why join Forge ?

Why Join Forge

Why Join Forge

Forge is a hardware-focused accelerator built on a network of manufacturing companies and large enterprises. It provides engineering support, fab space, PoC opportunities (implemented based on participating companies’ approval and the readiness of the team/product), and investor access, driving the process practically from prototyping through implementation to market launch.

01
Up to ¥2,000,000 prototyping voucher

We co‑fund your prototyping costs up to ¥2,000,000 so you can reach production‑ready faster and with less risk.

02
Investor matching

Warm, targeted introductions to aligned investors, plus hands‑on support for narrative, data room, and diligence.

03
PoC pathway

From scoping → field trials → integration with leading corporates—pending partner approval and team/product readiness.

How Forge Works

How Forge Works

Apply now

Apply now
Phase 1
Phase 1

Prep for Prototyping & PoC

Duration: 3 months — A focused ramp to get production-ready and PoC-ready: align milestones, start DFM and set manufacturability direction, design your PoC and success metrics, map stakeholders, and build momentum with regular mentoring, masterclasses, and co-working & fab access. Formal DFM lock happens in Phase 2 once the design is finalized.

What’s included

Refine prototype design and initiate DFM (set manufacturability direction).

PoC design: define hypotheses, success metrics, data & governance.

Stakeholder identification & engagement plan (customers, corporate partners, regulators).

Milestone planning & mentoring with industry veterans.

Masterclasses (manufacturing, supply chain, quality/compliance, fundraising).

Curated networking with potential PoC partners and investors.

Access to co‑working spaces.

Phase 2
Phase 2

Build & Prove

Duration: 8 months — Accelerate prototyping and advance validation and implementation. Co-funded prototyping (up to ¥2,000,000), production-oriented engineering (including formal DFM lock), co-creation and implementation with large enterprise partners via a partner-validated PoC track (subject to partner approval and team/product readiness), and comprehensive investor introductions and fundraising support.

What’s included

We co‑fund your prototyping—covering up to ¥2,000,000 in costs. (also availabe for POC)

One‑stop prototyping support to reach production‑ready.

PoC pathway with leading corporates (scoping → field trials → integration)—depending on partner approval and team/product readiness.

Direct investor introductions and hands‑on fundraising support.

All Phase 1 support continues.

Visa support

How Forge Works

How Forge Works

Apply now

Apply now
Phase 1
Phase 1

Prep for Prototyping & PoC

Duration: 3 months — A focused ramp to get production-ready and PoC-ready: align milestones, start DFM and set manufacturability direction, design your PoC and success metrics, map stakeholders, and build momentum with regular mentoring, masterclasses, and co-working & fab access. Formal DFM lock happens in Phase 2 once the design is finalized.

What’s included

Refine prototype design and initiate DFM (set manufacturability direction).

PoC design: define hypotheses, success metrics, data & governance.

Stakeholder identification & engagement plan (customers, corporate partners, regulators).

Milestone planning & mentoring with industry veterans.

Masterclasses (manufacturing, supply chain, quality/compliance, fundraising).

Curated networking with potential PoC partners and investors.

Access to co‑working space and fabrication facilities (fab space, tools, lab time).

Phase 2
Phase 2

Build & Prove

Duration: 8 months — Accelerate prototyping and advance validation and implementation. Co-funded prototyping (up to ¥2,000,000), production-oriented engineering (including formal DFM lock), co-creation and implementation with large enterprise partners via a partner-validated PoC track (subject to partner approval and team/product readiness), and comprehensive investor introductions and fundraising support.

What’s included

We co‑fund your prototyping—covering up to ¥2,000,000 in costs. (also availabe for POC)

One‑stop prototyping support to reach production‑ready.

PoC pathway with leading corporates (scoping → field trials → integration)—depending on partner approval and team/product readiness.

Direct investor introductions and hands‑on fundraising support.

All Phase 1 support continues.

Visa support

For startups

What
You’ll Get

What
You’ll Get

Joining Forge means more than mentorship — it’s hands-on, real-world support designed to move your startup forward.

Built for Global Hardware Startups

Designed for Japan, Ready for the World

Apply

Apply

🛠️ Upto 2M JPY in Prototyping Support

Refine your hardware with access to Japan’s advanced design, mechanical, and control systems — including CAD, 3D modeling, and fabrication.

🧠 Masterclasses & Mentorship

Learn from seasoned founders, domain experts, and Japan-market veterans. Sessions cover everything from local business culture to scaling strategy.

🤝 Investor & Partner Matching

We connect you with the right people — VCs, corporates, and partners interested in your tech and ready to collaborate.

📍 Tokyo Workspace

Access creative coworking and fabrication spaces in central Tokyo — perfect for meetings, building, or just getting things done.

🌐 PoC Opportunities

Pilot your product in real-world environments with support from our testbed partners in mobility, robotics, drones, and more.

who we're looking for

ELIGIBILITY

ELIGIBILITY

Forge is designed for ambitious hardware startups aiming to scale globally — whether you’re a Japanese startup looking outward or an international startup eager to tap into Japan’s deep tech and manufacturing strengths. You’re a great fit if

Product Development Stage

You have or are building a hardware prototype (MVP acceptable), are in DFM/engineering validation, or are preparing for pilot or early mass production; you can share core design files (e.g., CAD, basic BOM) for DFM review.

Tokyo focus

You are Tokyo-based, or you’re exploring Tokyo as a potential market and open to evaluating it during the program (market discovery, pilots, and/or incorporation).

Tokyo Is on Your Roadmap

You’re exploring Tokyo as a key market — or a launchpad into Asia and beyond.

Stage & team

Seed stage or later, with a technical owner accountable for product and DFM (founder, senior engineer, or committed external partner); full‑time preferred but not required.

Commitment:

A core team member can engage regularly throughout the program (check‑ins, design/DFM reviews; onsite sessions as needed).

PoC readiness:

You can define a PoC hypothesis and success metrics during the prep phase; progression to PoC is subject to partner approval and team/product readiness.

Timeline

Cohort 2 Program Timeline

Cohort 2 Program Timeline

Forge is designed to move fast — and move you forward. Here's what to expect:

Apply

Apply

Applications Open

March 1st - May 6th

2026

Submit your startup for review. No equity, no fees — just bring your vision.

Screening & Results

May 7th - May 15th

2026

We select high-potential startups through document reviews, interviews, technical and business evaluations with our manufacturing partners.

Phase 1

May 25th - Aug 14th

2026

A focused ramp to get production‑ready and PoC‑ready: align milestones, lock DFM, design your PoC and success metrics, map stakeholders, and build momentum with regular mentoring, masterclasses, and co‑working & fab access.

Phase 2

Aug 17th - March 29th

2026 - 2027

Build, validate, and commercialize: co‑funded prototyping (up to ¥2,000,000), one‑stop engineering toward production readiness, a partner‑validated PoC track (depending on partner approval and team/product readiness), and direct investor introductions with hands‑on fundraising support.

Mentors

Mentors

All Mentors

All Mentors

植松 千春

植松 千春

Startups

Forge Startups

The Forge program brings together promising hardware startups across diverse subsectors—from mobility and robotics to sensors, manufacturing technologies, and industrial hardware. These teams are building next-generation products with global potential, supported by hands-on business and technical mentoring.

Startups

Forge Startups

The Forge program brings together promising hardware startups across diverse subsectors—from mobility and robotics to sensors, manufacturing technologies, and industrial hardware. These teams are building next-generation products with global potential, supported by hands-on business and technical mentoring.

View all startups

View all startups

About the project

ABOUT TIB CATAPULT

ABOUT TIB CATAPULT

TIB CATAPULT is a Tokyo‑led initiative where the Tokyo Metropolitan Government partners with multi‑company innovation clusters to drive collaboration with startups across strategic industries and technologies. TSI (Technology Seed Incubation) serves as the lead organization for the manufacturing cluster “Forge,” accelerating co‑creation between corporates and startups and turning concepts into real outcomes.

Learn more

Learn more

Let's Work Together

Contact Now

Contact Us!

Contact Us!

Got questions about Forge or the program fit?
Drop us a message; we’re happy to connect and share more.

For Investors & Partners Get Intouch

hello@forge-tokyo.com

Looking to engage with the next generation of hardware innovators in Japan? We welcome collaborations with investors, corporates, and ecosystem partners who want to support and work with breakthrough startups.

Who we are

Program Overview

What is Forge ?

Forge is a global hardware accelerator backed by the Tokyo Metropolitan Government’s TIB CATAPULT program. We pair Japan’s manufacturing strength with committed corporate partners to move hardware startups from prototype to proof and into production—combining co‑funded prototyping, a partner‑validated PoC pathway (subject to partner approval and team/product readiness), and investor access, supported by veteran mentors, fab access, and rigorous DFM (Design for Manufacturability). Designed for seed stage and beyond, Forge concentrates support into two phases—prep and build.

Why join Forge ?

Why Join Forge

Forge is a hardware-focused accelerator built on a network of manufacturing companies and large enterprises. It provides engineering support, fab space, PoC opportunities (implemented based on participating companies’ approval and the readiness of the team/product), and investor access, driving the process practically from prototyping through implementation to market launch.

01
Up to ¥2,000,000 prototyping voucher

We co‑fund your prototyping costs up to ¥2,000,000 so you can reach production‑ready faster and with less risk.

02
Investor matching

Warm, targeted introductions to aligned investors, plus hands‑on support for narrative, data room, and diligence.

03
PoC pathway

From scoping → field trials → integration with leading corporates—pending partner approval and team/product readiness.

How Forge Works

Apply

Apply
Phase 1
Phase 1

Prep for Prototyping & PoC

Duration: 3 months — A focused ramp to get production‑ready and PoC‑ready: align milestones, lock DFM, design your PoC and success metrics, map stakeholders, and build momentum with regular mentoring, masterclasses, and co‑working & fab access.

What’s included

Finalize prototype design and DFM (design for manufacturing).

PoC design: define hypotheses, success metrics, data & governance.

Stakeholder identification & engagement plan (customers, corporate partners, regulators).

Milestone planning & weekly mentoring with industry veterans.

Masterclasses (manufacturing, supply chain, quality/compliance, fundraising).

Curated networking with potential PoC partners and investors.

Access to co‑working space and fabrication facilities (fab space, tools, lab time).

Phase 2
Phase 2

Build & Prove

Duration: 8 months — Build, validate, and commercialize: co‑funded prototyping (up to ¥2,000,000), one‑stop engineering toward production readiness, a partner‑validated PoC track (pending partner approval and team/product readiness), and direct investor introductions with hands‑on fundraising support.

What’s included

We co‑fund your prototyping—covering up to ¥2,000,000 in costs.

One‑stop prototyping support to reach production‑ready.

Partner‑validated PoC pathway with leading corporates (scoping → field trials → integration)—pending partner approval and team/product readiness.

Direct investor introductions and hands‑on fundraising support.

All Phase 1 support continues.

Visa support

Startups

Forge
Startups

The Forge program brings together promising hardware startups across diverse subsectors—from mobility and robotics to sensors, manufacturing technologies, and industrial hardware. These teams are building next-generation products with global potential, supported by hands-on business and technical mentoring.

About the project

ABOUT TIB CATAPULT

TIB CATAPULT is a Tokyo‑led initiative where the Tokyo Metropolitan Government partners with multi‑company innovation clusters to drive collaboration with startups across strategic industries and technologies. TSI (Technology Seed Incubation) serves as the lead organization for the manufacturing cluster “Forge,” accelerating co‑creation between corporates and startups and turning concepts into real outcomes.
